svg.title: QuickEats C4 Container diagram
svg.desc: QuickEats system boundary containing seven containers: mobile/web app, API gateway, order service, rider tracking service, catalog service, notification service, and analytics service, plus three external systems.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What architectural style suits the QuickEats customer app and API edge, and what quality attribute drives that choice?')
sendPrompt('What does the API gateway need to handle in QuickEats — auth, rate limiting, what else?')
sendPrompt('Zoom into the Order Service container for QuickEats — draw a C4 Component diagram with 3-5 components inside it.')
sendPrompt('What architectural style suits the QuickEats rider tracking service given real-time location updates?')
sendPrompt('What architectural style suits the QuickEats catalog and menus service, and how should it handle menu updates from restaurants?')
sendPrompt('What architectural style suits the QuickEats notification service, and how should it handle delivery failures?')
sendPrompt('What architectural style suits the QuickEats analytics and reporting service, given its read-heavy nature and spike tolerance?')
sendPrompt('What data stores does the QuickEats order service need — relational, cache, event log?')
sendPrompt('What consistency guarantees does QuickEats need from the payment gateway, and how should it handle a payment timeout?')
sendPrompt('How does QuickEats use the maps service for both rider assignment and live tracking on the customer side?')
sendPrompt('How should QuickEats handle SMS/push notification delivery failures mid-order?')
